_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL state-leg batch (SD-36/38 + HD-36/37/38): ileana-garcia (SD-36 R Latinas-for-Trump co-founder; YES SB300/HB5/HB1, supported HB1557, YES SB1718 — but 2A DEVIATIONS: only Senate R to vote NO on permitless carry HB543 + voted to KILL campus carry SB814, and 2025 statement condemning mass deportation as 'inhumane' → border q1 FALSE; ~67), rachel-plakon (HD-36 R; PRIME SPONSOR HB1521 trans-bathroom + HB1495 gender-identity-employment, floor-spoke for SB300, co-sponsor HB1 2024 online-minor-protections + HB601 back-the-blue, co-introducer HB543 + NRA Life Member; no deviation; 100), susan-plasencia (HD-37 R swing-seat WON 2024 by ~465; Freedom Index 76/CPAC 86; YES SB300, co-introducer HB1 ESA + HB3 2024 age-verif, YES HB3-2023 anti-ESG, YES HB1451 deny-IDs-to-illegals, YES lower-gun-age/SB214; no deviation; 100), alexis-calatayud (SD-38 R; CORRECTION — current file overscored 97 marking 'voted for heartbeat ban' TRUE, but she VOTED NO on SB300 6-week ban joining Dems [committed to 15-week-w/exceptions only] → life q0 FALSE; YES SB254 minor-gender-care ban, supported HB543 carry, ABSTAINED on SB1718; ~67), david-smith (HD-38 R USMC Col, 4th/final term term-limited-2026; ALL roll calls confirmed via FL Leg PDFs — YEA SB300/HB5/HB1557/SB254/HB1/HB3/SB1718/HB543 + NRA Life Member; no deviation; 100).

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-36 (Miami-Dade) Republican; co-founder of 'Latinas for Trump'; first elected 2020 (old SD-37, by 34 votes after recount), re-elected 2022 in SD-36 (59-41 over D Raquel Pacheco); not on 2024 ballot (staggered terms); running for reelection 2026 (D George Lavin filed). Named chair of Criminal & Civil Justice Appropriations (Nov 2024). Scored from HARD Senate roll calls + dated public statements. Notable MAVERICK profile: despite a pro-life voting record she is the highest-profile FL-Senate Republican on 2A and immigration deviations (see notes).
profile.background: Co-founder of 'Latinas for Trump'; communications professional. Elected to the FL Senate in 2020; chairs Criminal & Civil Justice Appropriations.

ileana-garcia@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Pro-life voting record WITH documented cross-party deviations on guns and immigration rhetoric. HARD: YES SB300 (2023 6-week ban) + YES HB5 (2022 15-week ban) (life q0); supported HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay'), incl. floor remarks for the bill (marriage q4); YES HB1 (2023 universal ESA school choice) (family q0); YES SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary immigration crackdown) (border q2). DEVIATIONS (HARD): the ONLY Senate Republican to vote NO on HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry) and voted in committee to KILL campus-carry SB814 (2025, 'zero regret') (self_defense q0 FALSE); and in 2025 publicly condemned the Trump administration's mass-deportation tactics as 'unacceptable and inhumane' / 'not what we voted for' (border q1 FALSE — opposes blanket mandatory deportation). Religious-liberty, economics, elections, 10th-Amendment, and discrete same-sex-marriage cells returned no HARD evidence and are null. NOTE: press framed her as the 'reluctant/tearful' GOP woman on abortion, but the floor record shows she voted YES on both bans — no abortion-vote deviation.

0.note: YES SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban, Senate 26-13) + YES HB5 (2022 15-week ban) — affirms protection of unborn life

4.note: No documented abortion-industry money; not inferred

2.note: No discrete biological-sex/trans-ideology bill vote isolated

4.note: Supported HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay'), arguing on the Senate floor for the bill — opposes promotion of LGBTQ identity in schools

0.note: YES HB1 (2023 universal ESA, Senate 26-12) — supports school choice

2.note: HB1557 mapped to marriage q4; no separate CRT/SOGI curriculum vote isolated

0.note: Chairs Criminal & Civil Justice Appropriations (governance role) — no affirmative pro-police advocacy meeting the high bar isolated; null

1.note: DEVIATION: in 2025 publicly condemned the Trump administration's mass-deportation tactics as 'unacceptable and inhumane' / 'not what we voted for' — opposes blanket mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens

2.note: YES SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary immigration crackdown, Senate 27-10) — opposes sanctuary policies

0.note: DEVIATION: the ONLY Senate Republican to vote NO on HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry) and voted in committee to KILL campus-carry SB814 (2025), saying she had 'zero regret' — opposes constitutional carry

Scott Plakon); re-elected 2024 (53.6% over D Kelley Miller); running for reelection (R primary Aug 18, 2026). Committees: Judiciary; Criminal Justice; Careers & Workforce (Vice Chair). Scored from HARD bill sponsorships, a dated floor statement, and an NRA endorsement.
profile.background: Wife of former Rep. Scott Plakon, whom she succeeded in HD-36. NRA Life Member. Elected to the FL House in 2022.

rachel-plakon@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Strong social-conservative; no cross-party deviation. HARD: floor-spoke for SB300 (2023 6-week ban; 'it will save lives') + co-sponsored CS/HB415 (2024 pregnancy/parenting resources) (life q0); PRIME SPONSOR of HB1521 (2023 'Safety in Private Spaces Act' / restroom use by birth sex; signed) (marriage q2); PRIME SPONSOR of HB1495 (2025 gender-identity employment — bars compelled pronoun use against birth sex) (christian_liberty q2); co-sponsored CS/HB1 (2024 Online Protections for Minors / social-media age restriction) (family q3); co-sponsored HB601 (2024 Law Enforcement & Correctional Officers, back-the-blue) (public_justice q0); co-introducer of HB543 (2023 permitless carry) + NRA Life Member & NRA-endorsed (self_defense q0,q1). Religious-display, economics, federalism, and a clean immigration-cell map returned no HARD evidence and are null (HB1589 driver-license bill too indirect to score the sanctuary cell).

set.office: State Representative (HD-37 · Orange/Seminole; first elected 2022, seeking reelection 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: HD-37 (Orange + Seminole) Republican; 2022 upset over D incumbent Carlos Guillermo Smith; re-elected 2024 in a competitive swing seat, defeating D Nate Douglas by ~465 votes (~50.3%); running for reelection (R primary Aug 18, 2026). DATA-CONFLICT NOTE: a single-county ENR page (Orange only) shows her trailing, but full-district totals + her seating confirm the 2024 win. set.office: State Senator (SD-38 · SE Miami-Dade; first elected 2022, seeking reelection 2026)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified 2026-06: SD-38 (SE Miami-Dade) Republican; first elected 2022 (54.4% over D Janelle Perez), in office; running for reelection 2026. CORRECTION TO PRIOR RECORD: the published profile overscored her at 97/100 with Sanctity of Life 10/10 marking 'voted for/sponsored heartbeat ban' as TRUE — this is CONTRADICTED by the hard record: she VOTED NO on SB300 (the 6-week ban), joining Democrats, after testifying in committee (in tears) that she is 'pro-life' but committed to voters to support only the existing 15-week ban with rape/incest exceptions. Life q0 is therefore scored FALSE on the documented contrary vote. Sworn Nov 2022, so HB5 (March 2022) predates her tenure (no vote exists).
profile.background: Educator; elected to the FL Senate in 2022 at age 26. Represents a Miami-Dade swing district.

alexis-calatayud@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 — CORRECTS a prior overscore (97). HARD cross-party DEVIATION: VOTED NO on SB300 (2023 6-week heartbeat ban; Senate 26-13), joining Democrats; in committee (Mar 20 2023) opposed it 'in tears,' stating she is pro-life but bound to voters to support only the existing 15-week ban with rape/incest exceptions (life q0 FALSE). HARD aligned: YES SB254 (2023 banning gender-affirming care for minors) — though she prefaced support for 'fully informed adults' pursuing transition (marriage q2 TRUE on the minor-care vote); supported HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry), speaking in favor (self_defense q0 TRUE). She DID NOT VOTE (abstained/absent) on SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary) — not a yes, not opposition → border null. School choice, christian-liberty, economics, elections, public-justice, and federalism returned only SOFT campaign language or no evidence — null.
